q: What is the primary method of multiplexing where multiple signals share the same transmission medium by occupying different, non-overlapping frequency bands?
Which multiplexing technique divides a high-speed transmission path into discrete time slots, allowing multiple channels to share the medium sequentially?
What is the name of the guard band used in Time Division Multiplexing (TDM) to prevent adjacent time slot overlap and timing jitter?
In an FDM system, what is the purpose of the guard bands between adjacent channels?
b: To prevent crosstalk and spectral overlap
c: To increase the total transmitted power
d: To allow for automatic error correction

q: Which multiplexing technique utilizes separate physical paths, cables, or space coordinates to isolate individual message channels?
What term describes a signal that has not undergone any frequency translation and occupies its original, low-frequency spectral band?
Which digital signal-encoding format alternates the polarity of the transmitted pulses to represent binary 1s, resulting in a zero DC component?
In which line encoding method does the signal transition at the middle of every bit interval, providing a highly reliable built-in clock recovery mechanism?
What is the name of the standard North American digital multiplexing hierarchy level that operates at 1.544 Mbps?
How many standard 64-kbps voice channels (DS0) are multiplexed to form a single T1 (DS1) carrier frame?
How many voice channels are multiplexed together in a standard European E1 digital transmission frame?
What is the primary function of an encoder in a digital transmission system?
a: To modulate a high-frequency carrier wave
b: To convert analog signals or raw binary data into a structured line code
c: To filter out unwanted high-frequency noise from the channel
d: To demultiplex incoming signals at the receiver

q: What is the standard frequency range of a basic FDM Group according to CCITT/ITU-T recommendations?
How many voice-grade channels are contained in a standard CCITT basic FDM Group?
What is the baseband frequency range of a standard basic FDM Supergroup?
How many voice channels are multiplexed together in a standard CCITT basic FDM Supergroup?
What is the standard baseband frequency range of a CCITT basic Mastergroup?
How many standard voice channels are combined to form a standard basic Mastergroup?
Which multiplexing scheme is specifically designed for combining light waves of different wavelengths in optical fiber communication?
What is the line speed (data rate) of a standard CCITT/ITU E1 digital link?
Which term describes the rate at which the signal transitions or changes states per second on a transmission line?
In a binary digital system using standard two-level signaling, what is the relationship between the bit rate and the baud rate?
a: The bit rate is twice the baud rate
b: The bit rate is equal to the baud rate
c: The bit rate is half of the baud rate
d: The bit rate is four times the baud rate

q: What is the primary cause of intersymbol interference (ISI) in band-limited transmission channels?
b: Dispersion and overlapping of pulse shapes
c: Incorrect sampling phase at the receiver

q: Which line coding scheme is characterized by adding a transition at the beginning of a bit interval if the bit is a binary 0, and no transition if it is a binary 1?
What is the main drawback of standard Non-Return-to-Zero (NRZ) line coding?
a: It requires twice the bandwidth of Manchester coding
b: It has no built-in clock synchronization during long runs of identical bits
c: It is highly susceptible to high-frequency attenuation

q: Which standard digital carrier hierarchy level matches the DS2 line speed?
What is the line speed of a standard North American DS3 carrier system?
How many T1 lines are multiplexed to form a single T2 digital carrier system?
How many T1 lines are combined to make up a single standard T3 digital multiplex link?
In a T1 frame, what is the dedicated purpose of the 193rd bit?
d: Parity checking for the entire frame

q: What is the purpose of pulse stuffing (justification) in asynchronous time division multiplexing?
a: To increase the audio resolution of the multiplexed signals
b: To synchronize incoming tributary data streams of varying rates to a common higher rate
c: To prevent the occurrence of quantizing noise

q: Which type of multiplexer allocates time slots dynamically based on demand rather than allocating fixed dedicated slots?
What standard voice-frequency (VF) bandwidth is allocated for a single analog telephone channel?
What is the total nominal bandwidth allocated to an FDM voice channel, including guard bands, in a standard basic group?
Which digital signaling standard is used to interface a computer (DTE) directly to a modem (DCE)?
How many active electrical circuits (lines) are defined in the RS-232-C specification for standard serial handshaking?
In serial data transmission, which signal line is asserted by the modem (DCE) to inform the terminal (DTE) that it is connected to a telephone line and ready?
What transmission protocol allows the receiver to request a complete retransmission of corrupted blocks of data?
What error detection mechanism appends a block of check bits derived from a polynomial division formula to the end of a transmission frame?
In which transmission type is there no common clock synchronizing the sender and receiver, requiring start and stop bits for framing?
What is the typical start-bit and stop-bit logic polarity configuration in asynchronous serial systems?
a: Start bit is low, stop bit is high
b: Start bit is high, stop bit is low
c: Both start and stop bits are low
d: Both start and stop bits are high

q: Which line coding format is most affected by the accumulation of DC offset (DC component)?
What is the primary function of a Scrambler circuit in synchronous high-speed digital modems?
a: To compress the dynamic range of the baseband voice signals
b: To randomize the data stream to prevent long runs of 1s or 0s, ensuring stable clock recovery
c: To filter out unwanted high-frequency noise from the local loop

q: Which term refers to the actual average rate of successfully delivered data packets over a communication channel under real operational conditions?
What is the name of the standard digital telephone trunk line format in Europe and Asia that supports 30 voice channels plus 2 signaling/synchronization channels?
Which line coding standard is commonly used for the DS-1 (T1) signal interface in North America to prevent clock drift during long sequences of zeros?
Which line coding format is standardized for European E1 systems to replace strings of four consecutive zeros?
What is the primary purpose of a codec (Coder-Decoder) LSI chip in modern telephone equipment?
a: To perform analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog conversions of speech
b: To combine multiple TDM frames into an FDM supergroup
c: To generate dual-tone multi-frequency (DTMF) dial tones
d: To route packets across the internet

q: Which term describes the absolute maximum information transmission capacity of a noisy channel under the Shannon limit?
What type of time division multiplexing divides a transmission line among channels by dedicating a repeating time slot to each user, even if they have no data to send?
In a digital carrier system, what does the term 'DS0' represent?
a: A single digital channel carrying data at 56 kbps
b: The base-level digital service channel carrying voice data at 64 kbps
d: An unshielded twisted pair network port

q: Which encoding format uses alternating positive and negative voltages for binary 1s, and zero voltage for binary 0s?
Which multiplexing scheme is considered the oldest and simplest format, relying entirely on individual, isolated physical wire paths for each channel?
What kind of signal is produced when a continuous-time analog wave is sampled periodically at discrete time points?
What CCITT FDM standard consists of five basic groups combined together?
What is the bandwidth of a single standard basic CCITT FDM Group?
What is the bandwidth of a standard basic CCITT FDM Supergroup?
The primary disadvantage of FDM compared to TDM systems is _____.
a: The need for complex analog filters and susceptibility to intermodulation distortion
b: The large digital transmission bandwidth required

q: Which of the following is not a typical application of Frequency Division Multiplexing (FDM)?
What type of transmission is logically equivalent to a dedicated private-line circuit but dynamically established over shared public network assets?
In telecommunication multiplexing, the term 'tributary' refers to _____.
a: A high-speed trunk line leaving a multiplexer
b: A low-speed individual input channel entering a multiplexer
c: The power supply circuit of a repeater
d: The frequency band of the guard interval

q: Which network topology connects all nodes directly to a central controlling hub?
What is the term for a communication channel that allows transmission in both directions simultaneously?
What is the term for a communication link that allows transmission in both directions but only one direction at a time?
Which of the following systems is considered a purely simplex communication system?
c: A two-way radio link using push-to-talk

q: What interface standard defines only the functional descriptions of serial interface circuits, matching the functional spec of RS-232?
Which protocol specifies the use of parity, vertical redundancy checking, and LRC block checking to secure data integrity?
What occurs when the sampling rate in a PCM system falls below the Nyquist rate?

Which type of digital line code represents binary data by making transitions at the center of each bit interval, where a 0-to-1 transition represents one state and a 1-to-0 transition represents the other?
What is the maximum specified cable length for a standard EIA-RS-232C serial interface operating at its top rate?
Which digital network standard combines digital voice, high-speed data, and video services over a single access line using standard 2B+D or 23B+D channel structures?
What is the primary function of a modem in a telecommunication link?
a: To perform multiplexing of multiple baseband signals
b: To convert digital data to analog tones for transmission over phone lines, and vice versa
c: To amplify weak RF signals in the local loop
d: To route packets based on MAC addresses

q: Which term describes the minimum frequency at which an analog signal must be sampled to allow complete, error-free reconstruction?
Which digital network standard represents a high-speed multiplexing protocol that uses fixed-size packets called 'cells' containing 53 bytes?
In standard telephony TDM, what is the term for the continuous repeating time block that contains exactly one sample from each multiplexed channel?
What is the name of the CCITT FDM standard that consists of fifteen supergroups multiplexed together?
What is the bandwidth of a single standard basic voice channel in a standard analog carrier system?
Which line coding method is specifically optimized for transmission over fiber optic links due to its lack of a DC offset and constant power level?
What is the primary technical reason for using Synchronous Digital Hierarchy (SDH) standards in long-distance trunk lines?
a: To permit extremely accurate timing and direct extraction of low-speed channels without demultiplexing the entire high-speed stream
b: To eliminate the need for any optical-to-electrical conversion
c: To reduce the required cable core diameter
d: To allow the use of low-cost copper wire

q: Which device is responsible for separating a composite multiplexed signal back into its original individual channel streams at the receiving station?
Determine the Shannon limit for the information capacity of a standard telephone channel with a bandwidth of 3.4 kHz and a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (S/N) of 35 dB.
A digital multiplexer combines four 100-kbps digital channels. If it uses a dynamic time slot size of 2 bits per channel per frame, find the resulting TDM frame duration.

A local area network has an absolute channel bandwidth of 10 Mbps. If it successfully passes 12,000 frames per minute, with each frame carrying an average of 10,000 bits of data, calculate the actual throughput of this network.

What is the total data transmission rate of a T1 (DS1) carrier system multiplexing 24 PCM-encoded voice channels sampled at 8 kHz, where each channel uses an 8-bit code, and 1 framing bit is appended per frame?
What is the total transmission line speed of a European E1 link multiplexing 32 standard channels, with each channel encoded into an 8-bit PCM word and sampled at a rate of 8000 samples per second?
Find the minimum number of quantization bits required in a PCM system to provide a dynamic range of at least 10,000 unique levels.
Determine the maximum data transmission capacity of a noiseless channel with a bandwidth of 4 kHz using a 16-level signaling code.

A communication channel has a bandwidth of 500 Hz. If the highest frequency component of the input baseband signal is 600 Hz, what is the minimum sampling rate required according to the Nyquist theorem to avoid aliasing?
If a digital transmission system uses a 4-PSK (QPSK) modulation format and operates at a baud rate of 400 baud, what is the output bit rate in bps?

An asynchronous data transmission system uses the standard ASCII character code (7 data bits) along with 1 start bit, 1 stop bit, and 1 parity bit. Calculate the maximum coding efficiency of this transmission.
A digital transmission system must pass a data rate of 56 kbps. If the channel is noiseless, find the absolute minimum bandwidth required to transmit this binary (2-level) signal.
A CCITT standard L600 mastergroup FDM baseband spans a frequency range from 60 kHz to 2788 kHz. Calculate the total baseband bandwidth of this mastergroup.
Determine the bandwidth efficiency of a QPSK digital modulation system operating at a transmission data rate of 10 Mbps over an RF channel bandwidth of 5 MHz.
Calculate the maximum data transmission rate over a standard 6 MHz broadcast television channel, assuming a noiseless channel and using a 16-level digital signaling code.
If the bit rate of a digital transmission system employing Amplitude Shift Keying (ASK) is 1200 bps, what is the corresponding baud rate of the line?

A T2 digital multiplexer combines four standard T1 lines. If each T1 line operates at 1.544 Mbps and the T2 line speed is 6.312 Mbps, find the rate of overhead bits used for framing and justification.
A 16-PSK digital communication system operates at a baud rate of 2400 symbols per second. Calculate the data transmission rate in bps.
Compute the time required to transmit a 4-character hexadecimal word (e.g. 11CB) using an 8-bit parallel data transfer interface if the transfer time is 1 millisecond per character block.

A standard PCM telephone channel uses 8-bit quantization. If the reference noise level in the subscriber line requires a Signal-to-Quantization Noise Ratio (SQR) of at least 40 dB, find the minimum number of quantization bits required to achieve this.

A standard basic FDM Group contains 12 voice channels, each with a nominal bandwidth of 4 kHz. If the guard band between channels is 300 Hz, what is the actual useful spectral bandwidth occupied by the active voice signals?

A T1 TDM line multiplexes 24 voice channels. If each voice channel is sampled at 8 kHz and coded into an 8-bit word, calculate the bit rate of the information channels alone, excluding the framing overhead.
Determine the duration of a single bit on a standard T1 digital trunk line operating at 1.544 Mbps.
A digital transmission system uses 256-QAM. If the baud rate is 9600 symbols/sec, calculate the output bit rate in bps.
An analog voice channel is sampled at 8 kHz. What is the time duration of a single TDM frame in a standard T1 system?
Determine the time duration of a single voice channel slot (containing 8 bits) within a standard 125-microsecond T1 frame.
A standard CCITT basic FDM Supergroup is formed by multiplexing five basic groups. If each basic group occupies a bandwidth of 48 kHz, calculate the total bandwidth of the supergroup.
In a T1 frame, what percentage of the total transmitted data rate is composed of framing overhead?
Find the maximum theoretical capacity of a channel with a bandwidth of 1 MHz and a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (S/N) of 15 (which is 12 dB) using the Shannon-Hartley theorem.
A local loop telephone line has an average SNR of 30 dB and a bandwidth of 3 kHz. Find the Shannon limit for data capacity.
If a T1 multiplexer runs with exactly 24 voice channels, and each channel is coded with an 8-bit PCM word, find the total number of bits per frame.

An FDM basic Group contains 12 channels. If each channel has a nominal bandwidth of 4 kHz, find the total bandwidth of the Group.
Calculate the baud rate of an 8-PSK system operating at a bit rate of 96 kbps.
Find the maximum theoretical capacity of a channel with a bandwidth of 3 kHz and a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (S/N) of 30 dB.
Determine the quantization SQR in dB of an 8-bit linear PCM system using the approximation SQR = 6.02 n + 1.76 dB.
A T2 digital multiplexer combines 96 channels of 64 kbps. Calculate the payload bit rate of the T2 line, excluding all framing and signaling overhead.
A digital transmission system uses 64-QAM. If the baud rate is 9600 symbols/sec, calculate the output bit rate in bps.
Determine the time duration of a single bit on a standard E1 digital trunk line operating at 2.048 Mbps.

A standard CCITT basic FDM Supermastergroup is formed by multiplexing fifteen supergroups. If each basic supergroup occupies a bandwidth of 240 kHz, calculate the total bandwidth of the supermastergroup.
In an E1 frame, what percentage of the total transmitted data rate is composed of framing and signaling overhead?
Find the maximum theoretical capacity of a channel with a bandwidth of 2 MHz and a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (S/N) of 15 (which is 12 dB) using the Shannon-Hartley theorem.
An analog telephone line has an average SNR of 30 dB and a bandwidth of 3.1 kHz. Find the Shannon limit for data capacity.
If an E1 multiplexer runs with exactly 32 channels, and each channel is coded with an 8-bit PCM word, find the total number of bits per frame.
Determine the line speed of an E1 system if 30 of its channels are used for voice, 1 for framing, and 1 for signaling. Each channel carries 8 bits, and the sample rate is 8 kHz.
In an asynchronous serial transmission, a character consists of 8 data bits, 1 parity bit, 1 start bit, and 1 stop bit. If the system transmits at 9600 bps, what is the character transmission rate?
A computer transmits files over a serial link using ASCII with 1 start bit, 7 data bits, 1 parity bit, and 2 stop bits at a rate of 9600 bps. How many 8-bit text bytes can be sent per second?
Calculate the dynamic range of an 8-bit linear PCM system where the smallest step size (resolution) is 20 mV.
If the Signal-to-Quantization Noise Ratio (SQR) of a PCM system must be at least 50 dB, what is the minimum number of bits required per sample?
Find the bandwidth of a basic CCITT FDM Mastergroup that occupies the frequency band from 812 kHz to 2044 kHz.
A voice band circuit has a signal-to-noise ratio of 30 dB and a bandwidth of 3 kHz. Find the maximum theoretical transmission rate according to the Shannon-Hartley theorem.
Determine the step size of an 8-bit linear DAC if the maximum output range is 10.24 V.
A T3 carrier system multiplexes 28 T1 lines. Calculate the total payload voice channels (DS0) supported by a single T3 line.
A digital transmission system uses a 16-level code over an ideal noiseless channel with a bandwidth of 10 kHz. Calculate the maximum Nyquist bit rate.
A standard fiber link has a bandwidth of 5 GHz. If a 4-level signaling scheme is used in a noiseless channel, what is the maximum possible data transmission rate?
A 16-PSK digital modem operates at a baud rate of 4800 symbols per second. What is the line speed in bps?
Determine the symbol rate (baud rate) of a 64-QAM modulator driven by an input binary data stream of 54 kbps.
Find the maximum capacity of an optical fiber link that exhibits an SNR of 20 dB over an active bandwidth of 100 MHz.
An asynchronous terminal transmits characters at 9600 bps. If each character block consists of 1 start bit, 8 data bits, and 1 stop bit, how many characters are sent per minute?
Calculate the bandwidth efficiency of a 16-PSK digital transmission system.

Find the code efficiency of a transmission system that uses ASCII with 7 data bits, 1 start bit, 1 stop bit, and no parity bit.
